entry_transition.notes: Camera rises from tunnel exit (M10) to clifftop vantage (M11), mimicking player's climb to higher ground. Natural upward motion reinforces vertical geography and sets up the 50-meter drop reveal.

exit_handoff.leaves_on: Player at cliff edge, body coiled in decision posture, gaze locked downward on escaping boat cutting white wake through churning river below. Engine sound fading. Tools visible but untouched — moment frozen before the leap.
exit_handoff.next_scene_should_open_with: Mid-air or water impact — player already committed, tool deployed (whichever chosen). Transition should feel like consequence, not hesitation. Water spray, motion blur, adrenaline peak.

mood_arc: Relief from M10's enclosed dread pivots instantly to open-air urgency. The waterfall that signaled escape now frames a new crisis: vertical distance vs. time. Awe collapses into calculation — beauty becomes obstacle.
visual_callbacks: Waterfall mist (M10 background) now dominates midground, connecting tunnel-exit reveal to clifftop perspective
Tropical greenery from M10's 'light at tunnel end' now frames foreground left/right as cliff-edge vegetation
Natural light quality (M10's 'flood of daylight') sustained — high contrast, late-morning sun, same weather system
rationale: Push-up transition literalizes the climb from tunnel to cliff while maintaining spatial continuity of the waterfall landmark. Exit handoff freezes on *decision* (not action) so M12 can open on *consequence*, preserving player agency feeling. Callbacks anchor the same jungle environment across both missions despite radical shift in elevation and stakes.
